description: Low homotopy of SU(N) and the colour-selection test (R5).

table.note: Bott: pi_3(G)=Z for every simple compact Lie group.

table.physical_role: spin-statistics enabler; Witten SU(2) global anomaly
table.note: pi_4(SU(2))=Z2 (used for spin-1/2, FQ2); 0 for N>=3.
SU(3) is the first with pi_5=Z (nontrivial WZW); but SU(N>=3) all Z.
UNSTABLE: pi_6(SU(3))=Z6 differs from pi_6(SU(4))=0 -- a genuine 3-vs-4 distinction, but with no physical role.

verdict: Topology (physically-relevant pi_3,pi_4,pi_5) does NOT distinguish SU(3) from SU(4). pi_4/pi_5 separate SU(2) from N>=3 only. The single homotopy group that separates 3 from 4 (pi_6) has no 3+1D role.
